title: fix(image): return 400 (not 500) when user-supplied image URL fails
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Return to sender
gist.blurb: We've polished up how the image API handles broken URLs. Instead of panicking with a 500 error, the hive now politely returns a 400, and we've fixed a quirk that was blocking Telegram images.
gist.summary: Unified image downloading across 12 models to correctly return 400 client errors for broken URLs instead of 500 server errors, while adding magic-byte MIME detection.
gist.impact: Developers will see accurate 400 status codes for invalid image inputs, and image-to-image generation now correctly supports Telegram CDN links.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations.ai bee mascot acting as a helpful mail sorter. A cute chunky robot with a monitor head is holding a broken picture frame (representing a bad URL), and the bee is gently pointing them to a 'Return to Sender' bin instead of a 'Server Panic' button.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny mail carrier hat, gently guiding a chunky monitor-head robot to drop a broken picture frame into a lime green (#ecf874) 'Return to Sender' bin. Soft pastel lavender and peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny mail carrier hat, gently guiding a chunky monitor-head robot to drop a broken picture frame into a lime green (#ecf874) 'Return to Sender' bin.
